mnemonics.title: MEMORYLESS PROPERTY
mnemonics.content: THE PAST DOES NOT MATTER - P(X > s+t | X > s) = P(X > t). Just like forgetting the past, exponential forgets how long you've already waited.

mnemonics.title: PDF FORMULA
mnemonics.content: LAMBDA E TO THE POWER OF MINUS LAMBDA X - f(x) = λe^(-λx). Think: LAMBDA starts with L, LEFT side of formula.

mnemonics.title: CDF FORMula
mnemonics.content: ONE MINUS E TO THE POWER OF MINUS LAMBDA X - F(x) = 1 - e^(-λx). Remember: CDF always starts at 0 and ends at 1.

mnemonics.title: MEAN AND VARIANCE
mnemonics.content: MEAN = 1/LAMBDA, VARIANCE = 1/(LAMBDA SQUARED). The mean is always bigger than standard deviation.

acronyms.expansion: Maximum Likelihood Estimation - method to estimate λ from data
acronyms.usage: λ̂ = n / Σxi = 1/x̄ for exponential distribution

acronyms.expansion: Probability Density Function - describes likelihood of continuous random variable
Cumulative Distribution Function - probability X ≤ x
Moment Generating Function - used to derive moments

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a hockey stick curve starting high at x=0 and decreasing rapidly as x increases. The area under curve from 0 to ∞ equals 1.
Think of waiting for a bus. Whether you've waited 0 minutes or 10 minutes, the probability of waiting 5 more minutes is the same.
Think of λ as SPEED (higher λ = faster events = smaller mean waiting time). Like a fast faucet (high λ) fills bucket quickly (small mean).
Imagine a light bulb. For exponential model, it has equal chance of failing at any age - no wear and tear.

analogies.analogy: Like customers arriving at a store at constant average rate - time between arrivals follows exponential distribution.
Like a radioactive atom's decay time - when an atom will decay is exponentially distributed.
Like waiting in line at bank - if customers arrive randomly at constant rate, your waiting time follows exponential.
Like flipping a coin - if you flipped 9 heads, the probability of heads on 10th flip is still 50%. The past doesn't affect future for memoryless processes.
